chunks.text: 2018-19 Archive of Special Events - EECS at Berkeley. 2018-19 Archive of Special Events. Berkeley ACM A.M. Turing Laureate Colloquium. Awardees of the ACM AM Turing Award—called the Nobel Prize of computer science—reflect on their time in Berkeley between 1970 and 1990, while looking towards the future of research and technological development in their fields.
chunks.question: What is the ACM A.M. Turing Laureate Colloquium about?
chunks.answer: Turing Award winners reflecting on Berkeley experiences.

chunks.text: BEARS 2019. On February 14, 2019, “The Future of Medicine: An EECS Perspective” focused on how EECS research is changing the future of medicine. It started with six broadly accessible lectures about these recent developments followed by shorter talks by some of our graduate student on a broader range of topics.
chunks.question: What was the theme of BEARS 2019?
chunks.answer: EECS research changing the future of medicine.
